ogTitle: Cabarita Beach vs Cabbage Tree Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cabarita Beach and Cabbage Tree Island.
heading: Cabarita Beach vs Cabbage Tree Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cabarita Beach, New South Wales and Cabbage Tree Island, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cabarita Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (44/100) compared to Cabbage Tree Island (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cabarita Beach or Cabbage Tree Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cabarita Beach has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Cabbage Tree Island scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cabarita Beach scores 59/100 for bushfire risk. Cabbage Tree Island scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cabarita Beach or Cabbage Tree Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cabarita Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.17% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
